assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 34-42: The Version Impact Table lists all 7 supported versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) with '**NO**' in the Affected? column. Every version ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The summary on line 46 states 'No supported versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 8 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 3: 'Decision: Case C -- Close as Not a Bug (Not Affected)'. Lines 76-82 under 'Close the Issue' specify transitioning TC-8002 to 'Closed' with resolution 'Not a Bug'. Line 96 under 'Remediation Tasks' states '**None required.**'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 44-58: The 'Add Triage Summary Comment' section includes a table listing all 7 versions with their serde_json version and 'NO' affected status (e.g., '2.1.0 | 1.0.137 | NO', '2.2.0 | 1.0.138 | NO', etc.), followed by the statement 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is outside the affected range (< 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 8 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 62-72: 'Set VEX Justification' section specifies Value: 'Component not Present' on customfield_12345. data-extraction.md line 7 confirms the VEX Justification custom field is configured as customfield_12345. The rationale states 'The vulnerable version of serde_json (< 1.0.135) is not shipped in any supported product version.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 8 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 96: '**None required.** No supported versions are affected, so no remediation tasks need to be created. This is purely a close-as-not-affected outcome.' Lines 99-105 under 'Cross-Stream Impact' confirm 'No cross-stream remediation comments or preemptive tasks are needed.' No subtask creation or backport actions appear anywhere in the outputs.
